questions.question: Which of the following is NOT listed as a ready‑to‑use HR template in the guide?

options.text: Company Home
options.explanation: Company Home is explicitly mentioned as a template.

options.text: New Hire Onboarding
options.explanation: New Hire Onboarding is one of the templates highlighted.

options.text: Expense Tracker
options.explanation: Expense Tracker is not listed; the guide focuses on HR‑related templates.

options.text: Job Board
options.explanation: Job Board is included among the suggested templates.

questions.question: Where can users find additional Notion templates beyond the sidebar picker?

options.text: The Notion Template Gallery
options.explanation: The guide directs users to the Template Gallery for more options.

options.text: The Notion YouTube channel
options.explanation: YouTube hosts videos, not a template repository.

options.text: The Help Center
options.explanation: The Help Center provides articles, not a template collection.

options.text: The Community forum
options.explanation: While community members share templates, the guide specifically mentions the Template Gallery.

questions.question: How does Notion’s ability to store pages inside pages infinitely benefit HR documentation?

options.text: It provides unlimited storage space for files.
options.explanation: Infinite nesting is about organization, not storage capacity.

options.text: It enables easy access to related documents by nesting them logically.
options.explanation: Nested pages let HR teams group policies, procedures, and forms in a clear hierarchy.

options.text: It automatically improves search speed across all pages.
options.explanation: Search speed is independent of nesting depth.

options.text: It reduces the file size of each document.
options.explanation: Nesting does not affect file size.

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of creating a database for company onboarding in Notion?

options.text: It automates payroll processing.
options.explanation: Payroll automation is not mentioned in the onboarding context.

options.text: It makes it easy to track every new team member’s progress.
options.explanation: The guide highlights progress tracking as a key advantage of an onboarding database.

options.text: It generates performance review reports automatically.
options.explanation: Performance reviews are a separate HR function.

options.text: It publishes job postings directly to external job boards.
options.explanation: Publishing job postings is related to the job board template, not the onboarding database.

questions.question: Which feature allows a Notion job board to be shared publicly with external candidates?

options.text: Publishing the page on the web
options.explanation: The guide states a job board can be published on the web in just a few clicks.

options.text: Exporting the board as a PDF
options.explanation: PDF export is not a live, public posting method.

options.text: Embedding the board in a Slack channel
options.explanation: Embedding shares within Slack, not publicly on the web.

options.text: Syncing the board with Google Calendar
options.explanation: Calendar sync does not make the board publicly accessible.

questions.question: Which of the following block types can be used to enhance HR documents in Notion?

options.text: Callout blocks
options.explanation: Callouts are useful, but they are only one of several options.

options.text: Quote blocks
options.explanation: Quotes are also helpful, yet not the only enhancement.

options.text: Heading blocks
options.explanation: Headings improve structure, but the guide lists more options.

options.text: All of the above (callout, quote, heading, plus dividers, icons, cover images)
options.explanation: The article mentions all these elements as ways to enrich documents.

questions.question: If an HR team wants to centralize all internal policies and make them easily discoverable, which Notion page structure is most effective?

options.text: Create a single flat page that lists every policy one after another.
options.explanation: A flat page becomes unwieldy and hard to navigate as policies grow.

options.text: Use nested pages under a ‘Company Home’ page, grouping policies by category.
options.explanation: Nested pages under Company Home provide logical organization and quick access, as recommended by the guide.

options.text: Store policies in separate databases without linking them.
options.explanation: Separate databases lose the hierarchical context that aids discoverability.

options.text: Keep policies in an external cloud storage service and link to them.
options.explanation: External links break the seamless, all‑in‑Notion experience highlighted for HR operations.

questions.question: How can a meeting notes database improve cross‑departmental communication for HR professionals?

options.text: By storing meeting information in a centralized, searchable database that all teams can access.
options.explanation: A shared database ensures everyone can find past discussions and decisions, fostering transparency.

options.text: By automatically emailing the notes to every employee after each meeting.
options.explanation: While email distribution is possible, the guide emphasizes a centralized database, not automated emails.

options.text: By converting the notes into video recordings for visual learners.
options.explanation: Video conversion is not mentioned as a feature of Notion meeting notes.

options.text: By restricting access to HR staff only, ensuring confidentiality.
options.explanation: Limiting access would hinder cross‑departmental sharing, which is the opposite of the intended benefit.
